skill.name: radix-ui-design-system
skill.description: Build accessible design systems with Radix UI primitives. Headless component customization, theming strategies, and compound component patterns for production-grade UI libraries.
skill.summary: Build accessible design systems with Radix UI primitives for React applications. Learn headless component customization, theming, and compound component patterns.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The scanner incorrectly detected: (1) backticks in markdown as shell commands, (2) words like 'description' and 'destructive' as cryptographic algorithms, (3) relative markdown links as path traversal, (4) documentation URLs as hardcoded URLs, and (5) React import() syntax as dynamic code execution. This is a legitimate Radix UI documentation skill with no actual security concerns.

content.value_statement: Create production-ready, accessible design systems with Radix UI primitives. This skill provides patterns for headless component customization, theming strategies, and compound component architecture for React applications.
actual_capabilities: Create accessible dialog and modal components with proper ARIA attributes
Build compound component architectures using Radix primitives
Implement theming systems with CSS variables and Tailwind
Add keyboard navigation and screen reader support to components
Structure design system with consistent component patterns
limitations: Requires React 16.8 or higher for hooks support
Does not provide pre-styled components (headless only)
Only supports React framework (not Vue, Svelte, etc.)
Does not include runtime state management

use_cases.title: Building a Custom Design System
use_cases.description: Create a company-specific design system with full control over styling while maintaining accessibility compliance.

use_cases.title: Migrating from Styled Libraries
use_cases.description: Replace heavy styled-component libraries with lightweight Radix primitives while preserving component APIs.

use_cases.title: Adding Accessibility to Existing Components
use_cases.description: Enhance custom components with keyboard navigation, focus management, and screen reader support.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Dialog Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a modal dialog component using Radix UI Dialog primitive. Include trigger button, overlay, content with title and description, and close button. Use CSS variables for theming.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new component from scratch

prompt_templates.title: Dropdown Menu with Submenus
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a complete dropdown menu using Radix DropdownMenu. Include regular items, separators, checkbox items for multi-select, radio groups for single-select, and nested sub-menus.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating complex navigation or actions menus

prompt_templates.title: Theme System Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up a theming system using CSS custom properties. Include light and dark mode support, semantic color tokens, and spacing scale. Show how to integrate with Radix components.
prompt_templates.scenario: Establishing design tokens for a design system

prompt_templates.title: Accessible Form Components
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a set of form components (input, checkbox, radio, select) using Radix primitives. Include proper labels, error states with aria-invalid, and helper text with aria-describedby.

output_examples.input: Create a dialog component with form fields using Radix UI
output: Complete Dialog component with Root, Trigger, Portal, Overlay, Content, Title, Description, and Close elements
CSS classes for overlay (fixed positioning, backdrop blur), content (centered, shadow, rounded corners)
Form with name and email fields using proper fieldset and label elements
Accessibility: Focus trap inside modal, Escape to close, aria-labelledby and aria-describedby

output_examples.input: Build a dropdown menu with checkboxes and radio groups
output: DropdownMenu.Root with nested Menu items, Separators, and CheckboxItems
RadioGroup for single-select options with visual indicators
Keyboard navigation: Arrow keys to navigate, Enter to select, Escape to close
SubMenu component for nested dropdowns with proper positioning

best_practices: Always include Dialog.Title and Dialog.Description for accessibility compliance
Use asChild prop to attach Radix behavior to custom styled components
Implement proper focus management: trap focus in modals, restore focus on close
Use Portal component to render dialogs outside DOM hierarchy to avoid z-index issues
anti_patterns: Overriding ARIA attributes - Radix manages these automatically, changing them breaks accessibility
Skipping Portal rendering - causes z-index and stacking context issues with parent styles
Not handling disabled state - always provide visual feedback and proper aria-disabled
Using default styles - Radix is headless, always apply your own styling

faq.question: What is Radix UI?
faq.answer: Radix UI is a collection of unstyled, accessible UI component primitives for React. It provides the behavior and accessibility without any styling, giving you full control over appearance.

faq.question: How is Radix different from component libraries like Material UI?
faq.answer: Radix provides headless components with no styles attached. Libraries like Material UI come pre-styled. Radix gives you complete freedom over styling while handling accessibility and behavior.

faq.question: Do I need to add my own styles with Radix?
faq.answer: Yes, Radix components have no styles by default. You must add CSS, Tailwind classes, or use a styling solution. This is intentional for full customization control.

faq.question: Is Radix UI accessible out of the box?
faq.answer: Yes, all Radix primitives include WAI-ARIA compliant attributes, keyboard navigation, and screen reader support. You should not override these attributes.

faq.question: Can I use Radix with Tailwind CSS?
faq.answer: Yes, Radix works perfectly with Tailwind. Many projects combine Radix primitives with Tailwind classes for styling. The class-variance-authority library is commonly used for variant management.

faq.question: What is the difference between Radix and shadcn/ui?
faq.answer: shadcn/ui is a collection of pre-built components built on top of Radix UI plus Tailwind CSS. It provides copy-paste components you can own and modify. Use Radix for complete control, shadcn/ui for quick prototyping.
